# Canonical Protocol: Software Governance & AI Guardrails
The Canonical Protocol (Law XX / 2026-PCEA) establishes 23 immutable Canons to govern software architecture and AI agent behavior. It combats "Vibe Coding" and Cognitive Debt by enforcing structural rigor and human cognitive sovereignty.

## Key Principles
- **Canon X (Layer Segregation):** Strict boundaries between Interface, Application, Domain, and Infrastructure.
- **Canon IX (No Ornamental Patterns):** Prohibits overengineering and "stylistic" coding; mandates the most direct architectural solution.
- **Canon XVIII (Cognitive Sovereignty):** Requires Liturgical Cognitive Annotation—explaining *why* code exists, not just *what* it does.
- **Canon XIV (Adaptive Cybersecurity):** Proactive, multi-layered defense and Zero Trust principles.
- **Canon XX (Agentic Coordination):** Standardized protocols for inter-agent communication and mandatory Agentic Decision Acts (ADA).

## Usage
The protocol includes an injection mechanism (`inject.js`) to apply these rules to AI agent configurations (`.cursorrules`, `.github/copilot-instructions.md`, etc.).

## Repository Structure
- `/laws`: The 23 Canons (law00 to law22).
- `/docs/congress`: Records of the Interplanetary AI Congress and Law XX ratification.
- `/architecture`: Normative dependency maps and architectural artifacts.
- `/template`: Templates for ADAs and canonical project structures.
